reasoning: The background states that the Secret Service is asking James Comey about a social media post where seashells spelled out "86 47" (which implies "get rid of the 47th President"). While this might prompt a standard Secret Service investigation to assess if it constitutes a threat, similar incidents with high-profile figures and celebrities (e.g., Kathy Griffin or Eminem) have historically resulted only in voluntary interviews, not arrests. Under the specified legal definition, a voluntary interview is not an arrest because the individual is not taken into custody against their will. Furthermore, it is highly unlikely that "86 47" in seashells meets the Supreme Court's "true threat" standard, making an actual criminal indictment for this post extremely rare. A separate retaliatory arrest by the DOJ by July 1, 2025 is possible but unlikely in such a short timeframe (one month). Therefore, the probability of an actual arrest before July 1, 2025, is very low.
